news.title: 美教育部启动新一轮教育资助计划
news.summary: 据美国DailyFly新闻网报道，美教育部宣布推出新的教育政策举措。该举措涉及联邦教育经费分配、学校问责制及学生资助等多个领域。此举旨在优化联邦教育资源配置，对美国基础教育质量提升具有重要影响。有关情况值得持续关注。
news.content:
National News

WASHINGTON, D.C. — The U.S. Departments of Education and Labor announced Tuesday the launch of a new federal grant competition aimed at expanding access to postsecondary education and workforce training programs, including registered apprenticeships. The Fiscal Year 2026 competition for the Talent Search Program is the first under a broader partnership between the two agencies designed to better align education pathways with workforce needs. [...] According to the announcement, the grants will be administered through the Department of Labor’s GrantSolutions platform, marking a shift toward centralized management of postsecondary and workforce development programs. Officials say the effort is intended to streamline services and improve coordination between federal education and job training systems.

The rollout also signals the start of the Department of Education’s 2026 grant cycle for Federal TRIO Programs, with additional funding opportunities expected later this year. The agencies said the collaboration builds on earlier agreements to share resources and reduce administrative overlap across federal programs. [...] Federal officials pointed to an ongoing shortage of skilled workers nationwide as a driving factor behind the initiative, emphasizing the need to connect students with training that leads to higher-wage careers. The partnership is expected to include ongoing guidance for grant recipients as the program is implemented.

news.summary: 据美国《教育周刊》报道，美国联邦教育政策出现新动向。相关政策涉及教育经费、学校管理、学生权益等多个方面。此举旨在推动美国教育事业发展，对美国教育系统整体运行具有重要影响。有关情况值得持续关注。
news.content:
The U.S. Department of Education under Trump’s education secretary, Linda McMahon, is pushing a “return to the states” policy when it comes to things like how they spend federal funds, assess student progress, and hold schools accountable. Decisions that previously were either driven by, or done in partnership with, the federal government are shifting to states.

Education Week recently looked at the myriad of changes different states are proposing.

Louisiana, Iowa, and Alabama, for example, all have either proposed or been approved to combine funds that schools receive directly from different federal programs into pooled funds that the state could direct.

# States Get Court Extension to Complete College Admissions Survey

March 16, 2026, 1:30 PM UTC

Sam Skolnik

Reporter

Seventeen states convinced a federal judge to grant them an additional week to complete an admissions data survey they say is an illegal attempt to track how race and gender are considered in the higher education admissions process.

Judge F. Dennis Saylor of the US District Court for the District of Massachusetts granted the states’ request for a temporary restraining order to move the deadline to complete the US Department of Education’s survey to March 25. The judge granted March 13 the TRO without prejudice to allow the states further deadline extensions or other temporary fixes “as justice may require.” ... WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Education Department is handing off a portion of its student loan portfolio to the Treasury Department, a first step toward shedding management of all student loans as Trump administration officials dismantle the federal education agency.

Under an agreement announced Thursday, the Treasury Department will take over management of student loans whose borrowers are in default, meaning they are months behind on payments. Those loans add up to about $180 billion, or 11% of the government’s $1.7 trillion student loan portfolio. [...] - 2026 Elections

news.title: 美教育部长就教育政策发表重要声明
news.summary: 据美国教育媒体报道，美教育部宣布推出新的教育政策举措。该举措涉及联邦教育经费分配、学校问责制及学生资助等多个领域。此举旨在优化联邦教育资源配置，对美国基础教育质量提升具有重要影响。有关情况值得持续关注。
news.content:
COLLIN BINKLEY

LOADINGERROR LOADING

WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Education Department is handing off a portion of its student loan portfolio to the Treasury Department, a first step toward shedding management of all student loans as Trump administration officials dismantle the federal education agency.

Under an agreement announced Thursday, the Treasury Department will take over management of student loans whose borrowers are in default, meaning they are months behind on payments. Those loans add up to about $180 billion, or 11% of the government’s $1.7 trillion student loan portfolio.

Advertisement [...] “The Department of Education has issued a dizzying series of rule changes that make it harder for borrowers to figure out what their options are on their federal student loans,” said Kyra Taylor, an attorney at the National Consumer Law Center. She warned that any errors in loan collection would have “devastating effects on families.”

Advertisement

The move is part of Trump’s campaign to shutter the Education Department, an agency he says was overrun by liberal thinking. Only Congress has authority to close the department, but Trump officials are picking it apart through a series of inter-government agreements that relocate the department’s operations to other federal offices. [...] Advertisement

The 17-page agreement outlines a stunning realignment of the nation’s federal student loan programs, which have been overseen by the Education Department since it was created more than 40 years ago.

The agreement “marks an intentional and historic step toward breaking up the Federal education bureaucracy and dramatically improving the administration of Federal student aid programs,” Education Secretary Linda McMahon said in a statement.

news.title: 美教育部发布最新教育政策动态
news.summary: 据美国《福布斯》杂志报道，美联邦学生贷款申请处理出现积压情况。受机构人员调整影响，大量贷款申请尚未处理完毕。此举旨在保障借款人合法权益，对美国学生贷款体系运行具有重要影响。有关情况值得持续关注。
news.content:
### Apple iPhone 18 Pro Price Detailed In New Report

### Moments During The 2026 Academy Awards When Conan O’Brien Got Too Real

The Education Department is struggling with student loan application backlogs for other programs, as well. The department has been unable to comply with a court-ordered deadline to process around 170,000 applications for Borrower Defense to Repayment for post-class applications in the _Sweet v. McMahon_ case. Department officials cited staffing shortages as the primary reason for the backlog; the department lost roughly half of its staff in layoffs and buyouts ordered by the Trump administration last year. The department is currently appealing the court orders to the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals and is seeking an extension. [...] You’re all set! Enjoy the Daily!

More Newsletters

“ED is not well prepared to smoothly transition borrowers into other plans,” warned The Institute for College Access and Success, or TICAS, in a blog post updated last week. “ED has also not completed the process of implementing the new income-based plan created in OBBBA, the Repayment Assistance Plan (RAP). They have not yet completed the final rulemaking process nor built out their systems to enable borrowers to apply for enrollment. RAP is set to go into effect in July 2026.”

The Education Department has provided limited guidance and no clarity for student loan borrowers who will soon be forced to change plans. [...] The new data released by the Education Department on Monday reflects ongoing progress in reducing the backlog of applications for IDR plans. The department had nearly than two million backlogged federal student loan repayment applications for the ICR, IBR, and PAYE plans earlier last year, but steady processing has gradually reduced the queue. Monday’s court filing shows that 576,609 IDR applications remain in the backlog, down from 626,412 applications reported last month. No borrowers received a discharge of their student loans after reaching their IDR student loan forgiveness eligibility thresholds, although the department indicated that may change in the coming weeks.

news.content:
The proposals come at a time when the Trump administration has promised to continue serving this student population, yet plans to dismantle the federal Education Department. Advocates for students with disabilities have also expressed doubt about the agency’s willingness and ability to properly investigate abuse complaints.

Hannah Psalma Ramirez has more.

## IN THE COURTS

PUT ON HOLD: A federal judge is giving a reprieve to colleges and universities that owed the Education Department a ton of data for a new survey the agency is rolling out. [...] SAVE UPDATE: Student loan borrowers are asking a federal judge to reconsider putting an end to a popular Biden-era student loan repayment plan.

An appeals court delivered a ruling that effectively ends the Saving on a Valuable Education program, commonly known as SAVE, last week. The group of borrowers asking the district court to intervene consists of the same borrowers who filed suit last week, alleging that they are owed loan forgiveness.

## AT THE AGENCIES

COLORADO DISTRICT FACES TITLE IX VIOLATION: The Education Department said Friday it determined that Jefferson Public Schools violated Title IX after an investigation into the Colorado-based school district’s gender identity policy. [...] The district has 10 days to resolve the violations or face enforcement actions that can result in loss of federal funding. The department is requiring the district to rescind or revise policies permitting transgender students access to female facilities and sports. The agency is also mandating the district release a public statement adopting the administration’s biology-based definitions of the words “male and female” and publish information on how to report or file a complaint of sex discrimination on its website.

Jefferson Public Schools said in a statement that providing equal access to programs and services for all its students, including those who are transgender, does not violate Title IX.
